raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing a complaint against American Express for improperly handling a billing dispute. I used my Amex card at a restaurant, but was incorrectly charged {$100.00}, even though my actual bill should have been approximately {$20.00}. 

Heres what happened : The bartender took my phone with my Amex card open and turned around to the register behind the barout of my view. I did not get to verify the transaction amount at the time of payment. Shortly after, I realized I had been charged {$100.00}, which was clearly not my tab. The bartender had accidentally charged my card for someone elses bill. 

I immediately contacted Amex and explained the situation in detail : that I was charged for a completely unrelated transaction. The representative initially asked what my actual bill should have been, and I responded that it was approximately {$20.00}. Even though I was disputing the entire {$100.00} transaction ( as none of it was mine ), the rep filed it as a partial dispute, leaving me responsible for {$20.00}. 

Unfortunately, the dispute was denied because the merchant submitted a receipt for the {$100.00} chargewhich matched the disputed amount, but was not my receipt. I had clearly explained to Amex that I was charged for someone elses transaction, and the merchants response did not include any proof that the charge was tied to me personally. My signature was not on the receipt, nor was there any documentation linking the transaction to my purchase. 

I tried to escalate the issue and requested Amex to reopen or reinitiate the dispute based on the fact that I was charged for someone elses purchase. They denied my request, despite the fact that the merchant did not prove the charge was mine, and my signature was not present on the submitted receipt.

This is a clear failure of the dispute resolution process under Regulation Z. Amex ignored my factual account, mischaracterized the nature of my dispute, and improperly accepted the merchants submission without verifying that it pertained to my transaction.
